observations.recommendation:
1.24 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee/Property Custodian to:
i. Formulate plans and strategize the complete conduct of physical
count of all its PPE, and prepared and submit the RPCPPE not
later than January 31 of the ensuing year in compliance with
Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
ii. Reconcile the RPCPPE with the records of the City Accountant;
iii. Ensure the report’s usability by including all the required
information in the RPCPPE;
b. City Engineer to provide the City Accountant with the location, details, and other
needed information to pinpoint the exact location of the road being replaced;
c. City Accountant to
i. Reconcile accounting records with the RPCPPE;
ii. Coordinate with the City Engineer to determine the road network to be
derecognized, then drop from the books the cost and the related
accumulated depreciation of the subject Road Network account;
iii. Determine the cost of the donated parcel of lands and prepare an adjusting
journal entry;
iv. Record the receipt of the donated and exchanged parcel of lands in the
books of the City;
v. Coordinate with the GSO and Custodian of the Land Titles of the City to
take effect unrecorded donations; and
1.25 We recommended further that the City Mayor create an Inventory Committee, who
will be temporarily relieved from all their regular duties, to complete the physical
inventory in three (3) months or less, and to prepare and submit the approved
Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days
before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full
implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA
Circular 2020-006.

2.19 We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Treasurer to coordinate with the SPIDC to generate a report or list
showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the
year, certify the list, and furnish the City Accountant at the beginning of
every year.
b. City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to
correct the account balances due to the aforementioned errors and non-
recording of the Discount on Advance Payments.
c. City Treasurer and City Accountant to reconcile their respective records
pertaining to the balances of RPT/SET receivables.

3.14 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Acting City Treasurer and other concerned offices to coordinate with the
suppliers and city personnel to inform them of the unclaimed payables,
otherwise provide certification to the City Accounting Office that there
are no valid claimants for the long outstanding payables, to facilitate the
appropriate reversion of funds.
b. City Accountant to:
i. Reconcile the Accounts Payable and Other Payables accounts to
identify if there are liabilities that were already paid but
erroneously recorded in other accounts;
ii. Evaluate the unclaimed obligations totaling ₱30,529,555.05 from
the GF that remained outstanding for two years or more. In the
event that no actual claim has been filed, whether administrative
or judicial, or payables are not covered by perfected contracts or
records, recommend for the reversion of the same; and
iii. Analyze the existence of those liabilities paid in excess of what was
booked, as well as the reclassification of erroneous entries booked
in the Accounts Payable account, and make the necessary
adjusting entries to ensure that the accounts are accurately
presented in the financial statements.

4.7 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to
immediately prepare adjusting entry for the error. Henceforth, ensure the
identification of the source agency and the purpose for such fund transfers
for proper recording in the books of accounts.

5.19 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Accountant to:
i. enforce the immediate liquidation and/or refund of the unliquidated
petty cash;
ii. stop the grant of additional cash advance unless the previous one
has been settled, and issue certification to that effect; and
iii. prepare adjusting entry for recording the amount in excess of the
established fund.
b. Accountable Officers to:
i. request replenishment of cash advances when disbursement reach
at least 75%, and
ii. refrain from submission of petty cash vouchers in excess of the
established fund.

5.20 We also recommended that the City Mayor designate Petty Cash Custodians who
will solely handle the petty cash funds for the various offices in the City,
and shall not allow the transfer of funds from the PCCs to the city officials,
instead, follow the appropriate procedures in the disbursement of Petty Cash
Fund.

6.16 We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. to submit the required certification;
b. direct the Department Heads to monitor and check the correctness of
the data entered in the DTR of the JO and COS workers under their
supervision; and
c. direct the City Human Resource and Management Officer to:
i. prepare individual contracts for each JO/COS worker which
contain the specific job description and the specific office to
where he/she is assigned;
ii. monitor and update the database of JO and COS workers; and
iii. maintain position description form of existing regular
employees, and consolidate such in the Job Description Manual.

7.18 We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. BAC, City Treasurer, City Budget Officer, and officials concerned to
comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184, and pertinent laws, rules and
regulations;
b. City Accountant and City Treasurer to stop the grant of cash advances for
procurement activities that should be undertaken through the appropriate
mode of procurement provided under RA No. 9184;
c. BAC Chairman and CHO to prepare and submit the requirements of
GPPB Circular No. 02-2021 in the future procurement of ancillary
supplies;
d. Direct the BAC and end-user/implementing office to undertake only
procurement activities in accordance with approved APP, and
revise/update the plan, if necessary, to include items not considered in the
original plan; and
e. BAC Secretariat to post the required information under Item 7.1 of GPPB
Circular No. 02-2021 for the procurement of Ancillary Supplies.

8.20 We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
a. City Accountant to:
i. coordinate with the City Treasurer for the return to the SAs the
unutilized/unused funds for completed PPAs and submit
liquidation reports to close the account, immediately;
ii. coordinate with the project coordinators for the submission of
MOA and other pertinent documents related to the utilization of
fund for proper filing and documentation; and
b. City Treasurer to:
i. transfer the amount of ₱1,590,925.99 to the BFP;
ii. return to the SAs the unutilized/unused funds for the completed
PPAs;
iii. immediately secure the SARO/NCA and respective MOA/MOU
for all fund transfers from National Government, and provide the
Audit Team a copy;
c. City Planning and Development Officer to:
i. prepare a revised implementation schedule of programs, projects
and activities;
ii. create a Monitoring Team that will monitor the implementation of
the fund transfer.

9.25 We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. Require the Local Development Council, City Budget Officer and City
DRRM Officer to identify and fund PPAs for Disaster Prevention and
Mitigation, and Disaster Recovery and Rehabilitation areas as these are
also important in disaster and risk management;
b. Direct the City DRRM Officer to:
i. procure equipment, tools, and supplies as provided under COA
Memorandum No. 2012-73;
ii. judiciously prepare the AIP for LDRRMF, using the prescribed
format, incorporating time schedules, availability of resources and
other contingencies to avoid delay in the implementation of PPAs;
iii. ensure and expedite the implementation of identified PPAs, to
strengthen the agency’s capacity in mitigating and preparing for,
responding to and recovering from the impact of disasters; and
iv. submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the
format on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
c. Direct the City Accountant to prepare and certify correct the Report on
the Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund on a monthly basis; and
d. Direct the City Planning and Development Council, City DRRM Council
and City DRRM Officer to provide specific, measurable, achievable,
realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs
appropriated for LDRRM Fund.

10.25 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. BAC Chairman to:
i. submit the contract within five working days from the time the
contract is perfected;
ii. adopt the new mandatory provisions in the preparation of the
Omnibus Sworn Statement and disqualify bidders that did not
comply with the required format;
iii. check the correctness of the computation of NFCC;
iv. require the bidders for the submission of the Statement of the
Bidder of all its ongoing government and private contracts,
including contracts awarded but not yet started; and
b. City Engineer to:
i. provide a copy of Suspension Order and Resume Order to the
Audit Team; and
ii. issue a Certificate of Final Acceptance after one year from project
completion.

11.21 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. CEIP Officer and OIC-City Treasurer to:
i. review the fidelity bond of their respective accountable officers to
ensure that the bond is adequate and sufficiently covers the total
accountabilities of the AOs; and
ii. to ensure that there is proper turn-over of accountabilities of the AOs
as mandated by law; and
2. CEIP Officer to:
i. provide safety vaults, cabinets, enclosures, and other
infrastructures for the AOs to safeguard assets from all losses;
ii. to require the AOs to maintain cashbooks or Cash Receipts Record.
11.22 Moreover, the City Mayor should provide the CEIP Officer a government
vehicle and escort to secure and safely deposit the collections of the office to
LBP.

12.5 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to:
a. clearly state on the face of the disbursement voucher the duration of the
implementation of the projects;
b. certify by stamping or printing on the disbursement voucher that
previous cash advance for the same purpose, project or activity given to
the SDO requesting cash advance has been liquidated and proper
accounting thereof was made in accordance with aforesaid Circular; and
c. support the cash advances with the required documents as provided in
the COA, DBM, DILG, GCG, and DND JC.

13.9 We recommended that the City Mayor and Acting City Treasurer adopt, and
process the enrollment to the ATM payroll system to lessen the risks of loss,
strengthen the controls in handling cash for payroll, and improve efficiency
in the treasury operations.

14.11 We recommended that the Local School Board, of which the City Mayor is
the chairman, formulate a strategic prioritization policy for the allocation and
utilization of SEF to Projects, Programs, and Activities that are compliant
with the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7190 and DepEd-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017. Also, require the immediate submission of the
lacking supporting documents of the aforementioned disbursements.

15.15 We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and City
Treasurer to stop the grant of honoraria and incentives in undertakings
without legal basis and are considered as an ordinary discharge of official
duties and responsibilities; and RATA to city officials who are not entitled to
such allowances.

observations.reason: Failure of the management to act on the audit recommendation
On-going implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Planning and Development Office and the City Engineering Office (CEO) to fast track the implementation of the planned PPAs to ensure their timely completion, and to optimally utilize the fund; and
b. CEO to (i) observe due diligence in the preparation of accurate and timely reports on the Status of PPAs, (ii) submit copies of contracts to the Audit Team as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-01, (iii) immediately submit to the Audit Team copies of Suspension Orders and other relevant documents to justify the delayed completion of the projects, and (iv) issue Resume Order immediately to deter further delay of project completion.
observations.reason: Please see related discussion in Part II, Finding No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Accountant to properly disclose adjustments and prepare necessary adjusting entries and (ii) reconstruct inventory records pertaining to the procurement of COVID-19 related supplies and materials and reconcile inventory with the accounting records.
b. City Treasurer to (i) immediately submit the liquidation documents on the cash advance balances, refund the excess amount thereof, and (ii) reimburse the BGCM Fund used in purchasing prohibited administrative equipment from General Fund.
observations.reason: On-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and Management agreed, to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents, and cancelled checks, if any, for purposes of review and audit. Henceforth, ensure that all disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents, copies of cancelled checks and other transaction documents are submitted on a regular and timely basis.
observations.reason: For CY 2021, various disbursement vouchers and their pertinent supporting documents were still not submitted to COA on a regular and timely basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to immediately submit the required supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers identified in Annex A, and to ensure that all pertinent supporting documents are submitted in a regular and timely manner.
observations.reason: Weakness of internal control
Please see related discussion in Part II, Finding No. 9 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. The CDRRMO to (i) submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Audit Team; (ii) submit to the Audit Team proof of existence and/or custody of the equipment procured enumerated in Annex B, provide this Office a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report; and (iii) use the LDRRMF Investment Plan format specified in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
b. The City Accountant to properly disclose adjustments and prepare necessary adjusting entries on the transfer of unobligated LDRRM fund in the Trust Fund books.
c. The City Treasurer and the City Accountant to coordinate in transferring immediately the cash corresponding to the unexpended year-end balance of the QRF from General Fund to Trust Fund; and
d. The City Treasurer to ensure that supporting documents are available and attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before the issuance of check.
observations.reason: Please see related discussion in Part II, Finding No. 5 of this report.
Please see related discussion in Part II, Finding No. 1 of this report.
Failure of the management to act on the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the: City Treasurer to immediately transfer to the BFP the balance for their PPAs; and the City Treasurer to return to the source agencies the unexpended balances as appropriate and the City Accountant to record the necessary entries.
observations.reason: Please see related discussion in Part II, Finding No. 8 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant to:
1. immediately require all responsible personnel to attach complete documentation to claims, and
2. ensure the validity as well as correctness of the transactions.
Management should strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation.
observations.reason: Weak internal control

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS provide the HGDG checklist to determine the actual expenditures that may be attributed to the GAD budget and GFPS and the City Budget Office to coordinate with each other to ensure that the approved GPB is aligned with the Annual Budget. We further recommended that the City Mayor re-constitute the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team that shall keep track on the implementation of GAD PPAs.
observations.reason: Please see related discussion in Part II, Finding No. 9 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed, that the City Mayor:
c. Refrain from granting cash advances to employees who are not
designated as disbursing officers, and without fidelity bond.
d. Require the City Accountant to be guided by the Revised Chart of
Accounts for LGUs in the recognition of government transactions.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct:
a. The City Accountant and all the personnel concerned to secure complete
documentations on all disbursements to ensure the validity and propriety
of the transactions. Submit the lacking supporting documents. And
strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation;
b. The Assistant City Treasurer and all LGU officials concerned to comply with
the provisions of RA No. 9184, and COA and GPPB JMC No. 1 to ensure
propriety and validity of the procurements, and that the City obtain the most
advantageous price in its procurements;
c. The City Accountant to be guided with the descriptions of the accounts, so
that the correct accounts will be used for each disbursement, and to prevent
the misstatement of each account.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Mayor require the:
a. LGU officials to comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184, and COA-
GPPB JMC No. 1 to ensure validity and legality of the transactions, and
that the city will obtain the most advantageous price in its procurements;
b. Accountable officers to monitor and liquidate their cash advances as
prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002;
c. Approving officials to refrain from granting additional cash advance to
officials and employees with outstanding balances unless previous cash
advances are fully settled;
d. City Accountant to attach a Certification that previous cash advances
have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct:
a. The City DRRM Officer to coordinate with the Budget Officer, and City
Planning and Development Officer for the proper presentation of
Investment Plan, maximizing the utilization of Local DRRM Fund, and
submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization on or before the 15th
day after the end of each month through the Local DRRM Council and
LDC to COA;
b. The City Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the
unexpended DRRM Fund for QRF and MOOE, and unrecorded donated
property; and ensure the completeness of the supporting documents;
c. The City Treasurer to transfer immediately the cash corresponding to the
unexpended balance as of year end of Local DRRM Fund for QRF and
MOOE from General Fund to Trust Fund and to secure supporting
documents before issuance of the check.
We also recommended that the City Disaster Council, through the City Mayor,
ensure the appropriate utilization of the Local DRRM Fund solely for
disaster-related activities.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Planning Development Office and the City Engineering Office
(CEO) to fast track the implementation of the planned PPAs to ensure
their timely completion, and to optimally utilize the fund; and
b. CEO to (i) observe due diligence in the preparation of accurate and
timely reports on the Status of PPAs, (ii) submit copies of contracts to the
Audit Team as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-01, (iii)
immediately submit to the Audit Team copies of Suspension Orders and
other relevant documents to justify the delayed completion of the
projects, and (iv) issue Resume Order immediately to deter further delay
of project completion.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor require the
BAC:
a. To review the contents of the bidding documents and see to it that the
bidders are complying with requirements of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 as
to the completeness of supporting documents, correctness of the amount
of the posted bond, and validity period of licenses and registration, among
others. And ensure that the submitted bidding documents by the
prospective bidders are clear and readable copies;
b. To ensure that posting requirements of the bidding process were
complied with, and the printed copies thereof are submitted with the
contracts; and
c. To be mindful of the deadline for the submission of the copies of the
contract to COA.

We recommended that City Mayor instruct the:
a. City Accountant and City Treasurer –
    i. to revisit the stipulations in the Memorandum of Agreement, Trust
    Agreement or Memorandum of Understanding between the City and
    source agencies;
    ii. to properly identify the required/applicable mode of disposition of
    the unutilized balance of funds transferred;
b. City Treasurer, to return to SAs the unexpended balance; and
c. City Accountant, to record the return of the unexpended balance.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Social Welfare and Development Officer (CSWDO) to coordinate
with the City Budget Officer and the Heads of Office on the preparation
of the GPB in accordance with the requirements of PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, and No. 2016-01; and ensure that it is aligned
with the GAD Agenda of the City;
b. CSWDO personnel assigned in planning, implementation, and
monitoring of GAD PAPs to undergo training on GAD, particularly
training program on the GeRL Tool, and seek the help of a gender
analysis specialist/expert to assist the City in catalyzing and accelerating
gender mainstreaming;
c. CSWDO officials to ensure that the GPB and GAD AR are correctly
prepared, supported with the required attachments, and attributed PAPs
were subjected to Gender-Responsive LGU (GeRL) Ka Ba Self-
Assessment Tool, GeRL tool tests, and other relevant tools; and
d. Accounting Office and Budget Office to integrate the RC codes in their
respective systems.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to secure copies of the Debit and Credit Memos from the City’s depository banks, verify all reconciling items thoroughly and prepare necessary journal entries to correct the deficiencies.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Treasurer:
    a.1.To set up a petty cash fund at the beginning of the year using the imprest system.
b. City Accountant:
    b.1. To investigate and reconcile the unliquidated petty cash advances and prepare demand letters, if warranted;
    b.2.Prepare adjusting entries for the unposted/erroneous postings;
    b.3.Ensure the completeness of the subsidiary ledgers.
We also recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor consider designating a Petty Cash Custodian other than the regular accountable officers.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor directs the:
a. City Treasurer to:
    1. Ensure that there is proper turn-over of the AO’s accountabilities as mandated by law.
    2. Ensure that all AOs are properly bonded.
    3. Assign specific cash advances to AOs within their maximum cash accountability to ensure delineation of responsibilities.
b. Accountable Officers to ensure timely rendition of report and such other records or their equivalents, as may be prescribed by the agency’s operating procedures, and to reconcile with the accounting records at least quarterly.
c. The EEDM Officer, in collaboration with the City Treasurer, to put in place the proper internal control structure for cash.
Moreover, management should set up a petty cash fund at the beginning of the year using the imprest system and designate a petty cash custodian whose sole responsibility is on the issuances and liquidation of the petty cash fund.

We recommended that the City Mayor directs:
1. City Treasurer to diligently monitor the granting of cash advances, submit the liquidation reports within the timeline, and immediately deposit the excess amount;
2. City Accountant to (i) ensure the correctness of the JEV postings, reconcile/investigate the under recording of the SAP Fund liquidation, and prepare and adjusting journal entry, if warranted.
3. CSWDO to (a) require the submission of documentary requirements for proper screening of beneficiaries, (b) ensure that the complete information are provided in the SAC card, and (c) ensure that the list of beneficiaries are listed in conspicuous places stated in the DSWD Memo Circular.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor directs the City Accountant to:
a. reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three funds and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement and make necessary adjusting entries; and
b. closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission.
c. whenever possible, settle immediately transactions between funds to minimize efforts in monitoring and reconciliation, and more importantly avoid erroneous account balances.

Moving forward, we recommended that the City Mayor directs the: 1. City Treasurer and the City Accountant to coordinate with each other to immediately prepare a cash analysis program, providing us a copy of the program, to ensure the availability of cash to meet the financial requirements of the City. We, likewise, recommended, and Management agreed, to utilize only Authorized Government Depository Banks (AGDB’s) for all its transactions, and maintain a single bank account for every Fund, including the Economic Enterprise Development Management for its special account, unless there is a justifying reason to maintain and keep various accounts in private banks and multiple bank accounts for the same Fund of the City, which should be properly disclosed in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
The CEO to:
1. prepare an accurate and updated list of ongoing and completed contracts; and
2. inform this Office of billboard postings before the start of the project.
We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the City Accountant to fully disclose necessary information in the original books of accounts and to submit the complete ledger accounts.

We recommended that the CDRRMO and Accounting Office to reconcile all continuing appropriations of the LDRRMF and properly monitor them until all projects funded therefrom are completed and that any savings be available for use in the disaster risk reduction activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.managementAction: Not implemented.
observations.reason: Private bank account still exits.

observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: Not all Cash Advances were fully liquidated.

observations.reason: GFPS still not functioning; GAD PPAs misaligned with annual budget

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to secure
copies of the Debit and Credit Memos from the City’s depository banks;
verify all reconciling items thoroughly; and prepare the necessary journal
entries to correct the deficiencies.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor:
a. designate a Petty Cash Custodian who is not a regular accountable
officer and direct the setting-up of petty cash fund at the beginning of the
year using the imprest system;
b. require the City Accountant to:
1. trace with the records, account and identify the accountable officers
with unliquidated petty cash advances and issue demand letters for
the immediate liquidation;
2. prepare adjusting entries for any unposted or erroneously posted
entries;
3. ensure the completeness of the subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Treasurer to:
1. Ensure that there is proper turnover of the AO’s accountabilities as
mandated by law.
2. Ensure that all AO’s are properly bonded.
3. Assign specific cash advances to AOs within their maximum cash
accountability to ensure delineation of responsibilities.
b. Accountable Officers to ensure timely rendition of report and such other
records or their equivalents, as may be prescribed by the agency’s operating
procedures, and to reconcile with the accounting records at least quarterly.
c. The EEDM Officer, in collaboration with the City Treasurer, to put in place
the proper internal control system for cash.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the
City Accountant to:
a. Enforce the immediate liquidation and/or refund of the unliquidated cash
advances and to refrain from granting additional cash advance to officials
and employees with outstanding balances until the previous cash advances
are fully settled /liquidated;
b. Designate a staff to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding cash
advances;
c. Provide assistance to the Assistant City Treasurer in the preparation of
liquidation documents of cash advances related to COVID-19 response,
and the Confidential and Intelligence fund, to hasten the process;
d. Perform the duties/responsibilities of the Accountant specified under Item
7.0 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and determine the unliquidated cash
advances that are non-moving for more than ten years where
settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained and initiate the
request for write-off as allowed in the same Circular; and
e. Send demand letters to those who were already separated from the service
or transferred to another agency, through their respective incumbent
head of agency.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee, to create Physical Inventory Plan, conduct of
physical count of PPEs and reconcile its report with the property and
accounting records;
b. General Services Office, to attach property stickers and to have a
uniform property identification system of PPE;
c. City Accountant, to properly compute the depreciation expense and
reconcile the misclassified accounts.

We further recommended that the Disposal Committee complete the disposal
process of unserviceable properties.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Planning Development Office and the City Engineering Office to fast
track the implementation of the planned PPAs to ensure their timely
delivery of services and maximum utilization of the same.
b. CEO to (i) observe due diligence in the preparation of an accurate and
timely reports on the Status of PPAs; and (ii) to immediately submit to the
Audit Team copies of Suspension Orders and other relevant documents to
support any delayed completion of the projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Treasurer to diligently monitor the granting of cash advances; to
submit the liquidation reports within the timeline; and to immediately
deposit the excess amounts;
b. City Accountant to ensure the correctness of the JEV postings,
reconcile/investigate the under recording of the SAP Fund liquidation,
and prepare an adjusting journal entry, if warranted; and
c. CSWDO to (i) require the submission of documentary requirements for
proper screening of beneficiaries; (ii) ensure that the complete
information/data are provided in the SAC; and (iii) ensure that the list of
beneficiaries is posted in conspicuous places stated in the DSWD Memo
Circular.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Accountant to: (i) prepare the required adjusting entries; and (ii)
reconstruct inventory records pertaining to the procurement of COVID-
19 related supplies and materials and reconcile inventory with the
accounting records;
b. City Treasurer to: (i) immediately submit the liquidation documents on
the cash advance balances, refund the excess amount thereof, and (ii)
reimburse the BGCM Fund used in purchasing prohibited expenses from
General Fund; and
c. Property Officer to issue Inventory Custodianship Slip to those in
custody of inventory items listed in Table 12.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor require the
City Accountant and other officials concerned to immediately submit the
disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents, and
cancelled checks, if any, for purposes of review and audit.

Henceforth, the City Accountant ensure that all disbursement vouchers and
pertinent supporting documents are submitted on a regular and timely basis.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct:
a. the City Accountant to: (i) determine the total unreleased share of each
barangay from the RPT collections, including the details of RPT share
recorded as TF and Due to other Fund; and (ii) prepare necessary
adjusting journal entries; and
b. City Treasurer (i) to release the respective barangay share immediately;
and (ii) to release all future barangay shares periodically without the need
of further instruction.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor direct the
City Accountant to:
a. reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three funds
and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into
agreement; and
b. closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that
these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error
of omission.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the Local Chief Executive
require the City Accountant to immediately submit the required supporting
documents of the disbursement vouchers identified in Appendix J, and to
ensure that all disbursement vouchers and pertinent supporting documents
are submitted on a regular and timely manner.

We recommended that the City Mayor directs the:
a. CDRRMO to (i) submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization on
or before the 15th day after the end of each month, through the
LDRRMO and Local Development Council (LDC), to the Audit Team
and (iii) use the LDRRMF Investment Plan format specified in Annex
A of COA Circular No. 2012-02;
b. General Services Office (GSO) submit to the Audit Team proof of
existence and/or custody of the equipment procured enumerated in
Annex B, and a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report;
c. City Accountant to properly disclose adjustments and prepare
necessary adjusting entries on the transfer of unobligated LDRRM
Fund in the Trust Fund books;
d. City Treasurer and the City Accountant to coordinate in transferring
immediately the cash corresponding to the year-end balance of the
QRF from General Fund to Trust Fund; and
e. City Treasurer to ensure that supporting documents are available and
attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before the issuance of check.

Reversions were made with SB Approval

Submitted the 36 vouchers and supporting docs

observations.managementAction: Adjusted

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (City Mayor) direct the City
Accountant to cause the preparation of the adjusting journal entries,
disclosing fully all the necessary details, and to closely coordinate with the
City Treasurer’s Office (CTO) on the bank documents required for
reconciliation.

We recommended, and Management agreed, to require the City Treasurer
and the City Accountant to coordinate with each other to immediately
prepare a cash analysis program, providing us a copy of the program, to
ensure the availability of cash to meet the financial requirements of the City.

We, likewise, recommended, and Management agreed, to utilize only
Authorized Government Depository Banks (AGDB’s) for all its transactions,
and maintain a single bank account for every Fund, including the Economic
Enterprise Development Management for its special account, unless there is a
justifying reason to maintain and keep various accounts in private banks and
multiple bank accounts for the same Fund of the City, which should be
properly disclosed in the notes to financial statements.

We reiterated that the City Mayor direct the:

a. General Services Officer and City Accountant to hasten the updating of
their records;

b. Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the properties of
the City and reconcile the updated amount with the Accounting Unit by
verifying any discrepancies noted, and prepare any adjustments to
present accurate PPE account balances;

We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the Departments
concerned with the local road network planning, implementation and
maintenance, to prepare and maintain the required records, and prepare the
reports as mandated in COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the City Mayor provide the Audit Team a copy of the
specific authority to enter into loan agreement with the DBP for the purchase
of heavy equipment and require the City Accountant/City Treasurer to
submit the documents supporting such purchase.

We recommended, and Management agreed, that the City Mayor require the:

a. City Assessor to prepare and submit an updated assessment roll to the
City Treasurer to enable the latter to prepare and submit the Real
Property Tax /Special Education Tax Assessment Register to the City
Accountant for the setting up of the accurate Real Property Tax
Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and related accounts at the
beginning of each year;

b. City Treasurer and the City Assessor to coordinate with each other for an
accurate RPT Account Register;

c. City Treasurer to submit the updated list of RPTR at the beginning of the
year to the City Accountant;

d. City Accountant to update and reconcile records in the books, and
prepare adjusting journal entries, if necessary.

We recommended that the City Mayor fast-track the implementation of the
planned PPAs to attain the envisioned socio-economic development/benefits.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the personnel concerned to
immediately submit the contracts for the two aforementioned projects and
their supporting documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor, through the City Budget Office
(CBO), the City Planning and Development Office (CPDO), and the
Sangguniang Panlungsod Finance Committee, considers the reversion of
prior years’ unexpended balances of 20% DF.

We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the officials concerned
to fast-track the implementation of the BuB projects, and to immediately
return to the Source Agency the unutilized balance.

We recommended that the City Mayor strengthen the coordination among
stakeholders by fully adhering to the Memorandum of Agreement and related
laws, rules and regulations on the grants from NGAs, to ensure that the
projects are properly and timely implemented; further, to cause the
immediate completion of the programs, projects and activities that are funded
by the National Government.

We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Treasurer

a.1. to immediately transfer to the BFP the balance for their PPAs;

a.2. to immediately secure the SARO/NCA and respective MOAs for all
fund transfers from the National Government, and provide the
Auditor a copy;

a.3. to closely coordinate with the City Accountant to ensure that funds
downloaded are recorded in the Trust Funds;

b. City Accountant

b.1. to immediately transfer all funds with specific purpose to the Trust
Fund;

b.2. to ensure timely preparation of the FURs and immediately record
the liquidation in the books of accounts;

b.3. comply with the regular reporting on the utilization of funds for
effective monitoring of the project from its inception to its
completion, to ensure timely and correct report preparation;

b.4. to return the excess funds immediately in case the unexpended
balances represent excess funds;

c. City Engineer, to justify the cause for the delay of completion of the
SALINTUBIG projects, and assess any liquidated damages, if warranted;

d. Personnel concerned, to facilitate, for fully implemented projects, the
preparation and submission of the required correct reports to the source
agency, copy/ies furnished the Auditor’s Office.

We recommended that the City Mayor require:

a. The CPDO, in coordination with the CEO, to ensure the submission, at
the beginning of the year, the list of all ongoing and still to be implemented
infrastructure projects for the year, as well as the completed projects for
the year.

b. The CEO to:

b.1 prepare an accurate and updated list of ongoing and completed
contracts;

b.2 justify/explain the reason for the non-adherence to the contract
timelines, attaching therewith any Suspension Order(s) if any;
otherwise, assess against the erring contractors the corresponding
liquidated damages; and

b.3 inform this Office of billboard postings before the start of the
project.

c. The City Budget Officer, to immediately provide the Audit Team a copy
of the ordinance of the substituted infrastructure project(s); and
henceforth, a copy of ordinances/resolutions affecting changes in
nomenclature of projects, without the need for demand.

d. The BAC, to submit an explanation for the errors/deficiencies in the
bidding documents and to immediately submit the lacking contract
documents;

We recommended, and management accepted, to cease the practice of
granting financial assistance to private organizations or individuals not for
public purpose/activities.

We recommended, and Management agreed, to require the City Accountant
and all responsible officials to:

(a) immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, together with their
supporting documents, and cancelled checks if any, to support the
recorded check disbursements, and

(b) ensure that all disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and copies
of the checks are submitted on a regular and timely basis.

We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the City Accountant
to:

(a) immediately require all responsible personnel to attach complete
documentation to claims, and

(b) ensure the validity as well as correctness of the transactions. Significantly,
management should strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without
complete documentation.

We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the City Accountant to
fully disclose necessary information in the original books of accounts and to
submit the complete ledger accounts.

We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the City Accountant to
draw up a journal entry voucher for the necessary adjustments in the books
and to reconcile the ledgers to their Financial Statement balances.

We recommended, and Management agreed, to direct the City Accountant to
compute the EWT in accordance with the Revenue Regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Accountant to prepare one
JEV and one DV for every check issued.

We recommended that the:

a. GFPS provide the HGDG checklist to determine the actual expenditures
that may be attributed to the GAD budget; and

b. GFPS and the City Budget closely coordinate with each other to ensure
that the approved GPB is aligned with the Annual Budget.

We further recommended that the City Mayor re-constitute the GAD
Monitoring and Evaluation Team that shall keep track the implementation of
GAD PPAs.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor require the
CDRRMO to comply with the guidelines on the preparation of the
LDRRMFIP and the Sources and Uses of Funds

We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a.) Accountant to prepare Adjusting Journal Entries to correct the
misstatements in the LDRRM-related accounts and to properly disclose
all required information in the Financial Statements.

b.) CDRRMO and Accounting Office to reconcile all continuing
appropriations of the LDRRMF and properly monitor them until all
projects funded therefrom are completed and that any savings be
available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management
activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.

c.) CDRRMO and Accounting Office to update themselves of the related
rules and regulations on the preparation of the DRRM financial reports.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare
adjusting journal entry to correct the misstatements.

1.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the:
a) City Accountant to investigate the reconciling items and update the
reconciliation within a specific timeline, cause the preparation of the
adjusting journal entry, and closely coordinate with the City Treasurer
on the bank documents required for reconciliation.
b) City Treasurer to follow up with the banks, copies of the Debit/Credit
Memo from prior years and immediately communicate with them for
any bank reconciling errors.

2.9 We recommended that the LCE direct:
a. The responsible persons to ensure that the payees immediately affix their
signatures on the payroll upon receipt of the cash;
b. The City Accountant:
b.1 to enforce the immediate liquidation and refund of the unliquidated
cash advances;
b.2. to send demand letters to the employees concerned to liquidate/ settle
the long outstanding cash advances, otherwise withhold the salaries of
these employees;
b.3. to refrain from granting additional cash advance to officials and
employees with outstanding advances until previous cash advances are
fully settled /liquidated; and
b.4. to appoint a staff to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding
cash advances
Further, we recommended for the LCE to seek authority from the COA
Chairman on the dropping from the books of outstanding balance from the
deceased former mayor.

3.8 We recommended that the LCE cause the liquidation of the IF/CF cash
advances and direct the person(s) concerned to prepare a Physical and Financial
Plan for the IF/CF in compliance with COA Circular No. 2003-03 and JMC No.
2015-01.

4.11 We recommended that the LCE require the Inventory Committee to
properly conduct the inventory taking, prepare a correct report thereof, and
reconcile its report with the records of the accounting.
4.12 We further recommended that the Management should facilitate the
disposal of properties which were already returned to the GSO and reported as
unserviceable. On the other hand, the CAO should prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of each PPE account.

5.8 We recommended that the LCE require the City Accountant and all
responsible officials to immediately submit the DVs together with their supporting
documents, and cancelled checks if any, to support the recorded check
disbursements. This is without prejudice to any notices of suspension, charges or
disallowances that may be issued as a result of our audit.
5.9 Ensure that all DVs, pertinent supporting documents and copies of the
checks be submitted on a regular and timely basis.

6.6 We recommended that the LCE require all claimants to attach complete
documentations on every claim and the CAO personnel in-charge to strictly
scrutinize the completeness of the supporting documents to ensure the validity as
well as correctness of the transactions. Significantly, management should strictly
adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation.

8.10 We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the necessary
adjustments to capitalize the borrowing cost to the qualifying assets, and disclose
the amount capitalized during the period. Ensure that all future borrowings are
properly recorded as part of the cost of the asset.

9.17 We recommended that:
a.) The LCE closely oversee the operations of the EEDM Office, and the
linkages of all reports from the EEDM Office up to the generation of
the accounting reports to ensure consistent and reconciled data;
b.) The EEDM Office Head should review the systems and procedures of
the enterprise operations, prepare and communicate reports of the
EEDM Office on a timely and regular basis and install necessary
changes for its operational efficiency;
c.) The Management cause the review of the software collection system
and ensure that its features serve its purpose, e.g., features on the
importing and exporting of reports from/to the system, to avoid
redundancy/inefficiency of work; and,
d.) The LGU be guided by the DBM Manual on the Setting Up and
Operations of Local Economic Enterprises (2016).

10.17 We recommended that the LCE direct the City Treasurer and the City
Accountant to strictly comply with the correct procedure in the collections and
recording of all Business Taxes. We likewise recommended that the CTO provides
a computation and attach the documentary evidence on the business tax refunds.

11.8 We recommended that the LCE direct the City Accountant to:
a.) record expenses in their appropriate account for which specific
accounts have been provided in the Chart of Accounts in order to fairly
present the accounts in the Financial Statement of the City;
b.) immediately submit supporting documents for all adjustments to Other
MOE;
c.) ensure that all prospective expenses are not immediately pooled in the
Other MOE, but should instead be classified in their specific accounts
in order for the users to have informed decisions in the allocation of
their scarce resources.

12.5 We recommended that Management comply with the requirements on the
posting of signboards in order to promote transparency, accountability, and good
governance.

13.6 We recommended that the LCE direct the City Accountant to:
a. Immediately prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the Other Deferred
Income to its correct account for all retention fees collected.
b. Ensure that all related receipts are credited to Guaranty Deposits Payable.

14.9 We recommended that the City immediately stop the erroneous
computation of the infra costing and that the LCE instruct the City Engineering
Office (CEO) to adopt the guidelines under the DPWH DO No. 197 and any
pertinent guidelines to arrive at an ABC most advantageous to the government.

15.9 We recommended that the Management direct the Budget Officer to
properly coordinate with various departments on the preparation of the annual
budget with the PPAs properly aligned with the City’s LDIP, and any changes be
brought up with the City Legislative for the proper issuances of
ordinances/resolutions, as the case may be.
15.10 Moreover, be guided by the CDP Guide and Concise Illustrative Guide for
the Preparation, Review, Monitoring and Updating of the CDP and LDIP issued by
the DILG, and the Manual for Provincial/Local Planning and Expenditure
Management issued by the NEDA on the processes for formulating the foregoing
development plans and investment programs.

16.7 We recommended that Management direct the monitoring team to look
into the status and conditions of the project(s) to determine the cause of delay or
non-implementation.

16.8 We further recommended that the LCE direct the City Accountant:
a. To properly liquidate the amount granted by the national agencies and
refund the excess funds in compliance with the MOA and Sec. 4.9 of COA
Circular No. 94-013;
b. To update the account balances of the respective Subsidiary Ledgers
(SLs);
c. To provide a copy of the Memorandum of Agreements on all funds
downloaded from the National Government;
d. To properly coordinate with the City Planning Division Office (CPDO)
and the CEO on the implementation of the projects.

17.6 We recommended that the Local School Board, through its focal person,
prepare the SEF Budget in CY 2019 in accordance with DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint
Circular No. 1 s. 2017. If the CY 2019 budget is already prepared, revisions should
be done through budget amendments.

18.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the City
Treasurer to improve/strengthen its collections by sending demand letters to
delinquent taxpayers.

19.5 We recommend that the City Accountant immediately prepare the adjusting
journal entry (AJE) to correct the liabilities and cash accounts on unremitted taxes,
and remit the same immediately.

20.7 We recommended that the City strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular
No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the said reports to
the COA Audit Team.

21.6 We recommended that the LCE:
a) Issue an appropriate policy directive in the proper implementation of
the GPB.
b) Direct the GFPS executive committee to conduct their duties and
responsibilities in accordance with the mandates to ensure that GAD
programs, projects and activities are implemented as planned in order
to attain the GAD objectives for which funds were provided.

22.8 We recommended that the City Accountant impose the mandatory NTHP
to all City employees to ensure that they bring home a decent portion of their salary,
thus properly motivating them to perform better.

23.10 We recommended that the LCE directs the City Accountant to:
a. Review the transactions and determine the taxes due and for remittance
to the BIR to avoid penal sanctions.
b. Submit the documentary evidence to show proof that the taxes withheld
have been remitted to the BIR.
c. Strictly adhere to the withholding and remittance provisions.

24.15 We recommended that the LCE, with the concurrence of the Sanggunian
Panlungsod, establish a DRRM Organization Structure and DRRM Infrastructure
that is compliant with the provisions of NDRRM, DILG, DBM and CSC JMC No.
2014-1, thus addressing the mandate of the Office for which it was created.

25.14 We recommended that the LCE require the CDRRMO to:
a. comply with the guidelines on the preparation of the LDRRMFIP
(Appendix O) and the LDRRM Sources and Utilization of Funds
(Appendix P);
b. ensure that the target for the budget year are properly quantified in
terms of its funding allocation;
c. update themselves of the related rules and regulations on the
preparation of the DRRM financial reports

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
1. Oblige the Accountable Officers (AOs) concerned to liquidate the
balances of their cash advances immediately;
2. Require the Accountant to verify the composition of the Advances for
Payroll account and prepare the necessary journal entries to correct its
balance and the balances of other related accounts in the financial statements,
and to see to it that no additional cash advances will be granted to said AOs
unless they have fully liquidated their cash advances; and
3. Strictly enforce the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-City Accountant to
prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the deficiencies.

We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to prepare the
necessary journal entries to: (a) correct the erroneous recording; (b) reclassify
the completed projects to the appropriate infrastructure assets account, and;
(c) correct the understatement in the contract costs of the projects -
Construction of Nijaga Park (Phase II) and Construction of Boardwalk
(Phase I).

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-City Accountant and
the City Treasurer to verify the composition of the beginning balances of the
Due to BIR accounts under the General Fund, Special Education Fund and
Trust Fund, and remit immediately to BIR all taxes withheld as of December
31, 2017.

Henceforth, remit to BIR taxes withheld from payments to
contractors/suppliers and from compensation of employees pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of Revenue Regulation Nos. 16-2005 and 17-2003.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-City Accountant and
the City Treasurer to verify the composition of the beginning balances of the
Due to GSIS accounts under the GF and SEF, and remit immediately the
amount of P6,770,889.04 due to GSIS as of December 31, 2017.

Henceforth, remit to GSIS the employees’ and government agency’s
contributions, as well as the loan amortizations promptly, pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 8291 and its IRR.

We recommended that the City strictly enforce compliance with Section
6 of Annex E of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 in the implementation of
infrastructure projects, particularly on the withholding of 10% retention
money from the progress payments to contractors.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-City Accountant to
require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice/s of
Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors concerned to post the
required warranty securities.

Henceforth, the City should strictly enforce compliance with the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects,
particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the OIC-City Accountant and
the City Treasurer to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts
pursuant to the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445, RA No. 7160, COA
Circular No. 95-006 and within the period prescribed in COA Circular No.
2009-006.

We recommended that the Office of the City Engineer and/or Project
Engineer strictly enforce compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and
its RIRR and the monitoring in the implementation of the projects to ensure
that they are completed satisfactorily and on time; and see to it that the
corresponding liquidated damages are deducted from the claims of the
contractors concerned, where appropriate.

We recommended that the LCE ensure the full utilization of the 20%
DF as prescribed in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Engineer, the City
Health Officer, the Chief of the Solid Waste Management Office and the
Barangay Officials concerned to undertake the necessary corrective measures
concerning the operation and maintenance of the EWS Project to ensure that
it will provide sufficient and safe potable water to the residents of the place.

We recommended that the Local School Board, of which the LCE is the
chairman, formulate a strategic prioritization policy for the allocation and
utilization of SEF to Projects, Programs, and Activities that are geared toward
building a sturdy foundation for quality education as enumerated in the
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017. Also, require the
immediate submission of the liquidation reports on the cash advances totaling
P4,718,807.00.

We recommend that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant be
required to submit immediately the one hundred fifty (150) disbursement
vouchers, together with their supporting documents to support the recorded
check disbursements totaling P94,609,335.83. Henceforth, strictly observe the
timely submission of accounts pursuant to Section 347 RA 7160 and Section
7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that all insurable physical assets be provided with
adequate insurance coverage with the GIF of the GSIS so that the City will be
indemnified or compensated in case subject assets will suffer any damage or
loss due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other fortuitous event. Likewise, require
the City General Services Office to prepare and submit to the nearest GSIS
branch office an inventory report of all insurable physical assets using the
prescribed format of the GSIS.

We recommend that the BAC and BAC Secretariat be required to
strictly observe all the procurement processes in the conduct of competitive
bidding as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of
R.A. 9184 and submit the lacking procurement documents. Likewise, require
them to strictly observe the timely submission of perfected contracts pursuant
to COA Circular No. 2009-001, dated February 12, 2009.

We recommend that the City Treasurer be required to immediately
transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF to the Special Trust
Fund, and for the concerned offices to prepare the LDRRMFIP annually and
to submit to the audit team the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF.

We recommend that the Local School Board (LSB) give priority to the
repair and maintenance of school building in the preparation of the local
school board budget as required in Sections 99, 100 (c) and 272 of RA 7160,
and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, series of 1998 and 01-B, s.
2001.

We recommend that the City Engineering Office carry out sufficient
detailed engineering activities and ensure that site location issues are resolved
prior to procurement of infrastructure project so as not to hamper the
implementation of the projects.

We recommend that management require the concerned contractors to
submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee or a surety
bond of equivalent value, to support the advance payments given to them as
required in Section 4, Annex E of RA 9184.

We recommend that the concerned Accountable Officers be required to
liquidate immediately their outstanding petty cash advances, otherwise
suspend their salaries until all cash advances are liquidated pursuant to
Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. In no case
shall additional cash advance be granted to them, unless the previous petty
cash advances given to them are first settled or a proper accounting thereof is
made pursuant to Section 4.1.2 of the same Circular.

We recommend that the concerned officials and employees be required to
submit the travel order and itinerary of travels as required by COA
Circular 96-004.

We recommend that all revenue collectors be required to turn over the
full amount of their collections at the close of business day to the
Liquidating Officer and for the latter to conduct a periodic count of the
accountable forms on the hands of the revenue collectors, pursuant to
Section 307 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 60 of the Local Treasury
Operations Manual.

We recommend for the full enforcement of the COA Order of Execution
by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the person
liable in satisfaction of the disallowance pursuant to Section 7.1.3 COA
Circular 2009-006 dated September 156, 2009.

We recommend that the City formulate an action plan to implement the
audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished
AAPSI form to the audit team, copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee
on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance as mandated by Section
96 of RA 10171.

We recommend that the concerned officials and employees be required to liquidate their outstanding cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, otherwise, suspend the salaries of the accountable officials and employees until all cash advances are fully liquidated pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Confidential & Intelligence Fund totaling P42,000,000.00 was liquidated in 2016.
observations.reason: Management’s leniency in the grant and liquidation of cash advances

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City carry out sufficient detailed engineering activities and ensure that road right of way issues are resolved prior to procurement of infrastructure project so as not to hamper the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The four (4) infrastructure projects were already completed

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City submit to the DILG Regional Office the GAD Plan and Budget for approval to make sure the relevance of GAD PPAs to the identified gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Submitted GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City adhere to the provisions of Section 100 (c) of RA 7160 by giving priority for the repair and maintenance of school buildings so that students will not be deprived of the facilities that are conducive to learning.
observations.managementAction: Please see related discussion on Finding No. 5 of Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City strictly comply with the provision of Section 3.0 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by charging to the Quick Response Fund (QRF), only projects that were damaged by calamities during the budget year. Likewise, require the CDRRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in order to monitor the status of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management maintain adequate internal control on cash by providing the liquidating officer a safe deposit box or vault, where to keep undeposited collections, cash book and unused accountable forms. Moreover, provide a guard and/ or watchman to secure the office during and after office hours. Further, devise some measures to secure the physical structure of the office to provide protection against various risks.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Accountant be required to transfer the unexpended balance for the Quick Response Fund of the LDRRMF amounting to P8,242,764.22 to a Special Trust Fund. Likewise, require the BAC Secretariat to submit copies of contracts for the procurement of infrastructure projects totaling P125,514,952.29 and each of all the documents forming part thereof, within five (5) days from the execution of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and all other concerned to immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers, together with the supporting documents for audit.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some Disbursement Vouchers
observations.reason: Please see discussion on Finding No.1 of Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the concerned officers to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances. This is without prejudice to the filing of the appropriate criminal and administrative cases against them as provided for in Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Confidential & Intelligence Fund of Former City Mayor Sarmiento was liquidated in the amount of P42,000,000.00, and other accountable officers liquidated P902,110.77 of their cash advances

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe COA Circular 2014-001 by implementing program and projects that are GAD related in order to address the basic needs of the poor sector of the City and assist women and children that are victims of violence.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor direct the Chief of the City Solid Waste Management Office to increase the awareness of the residents on the segregation of waste. The City Solid Waste Management Office is intensifying its campaign on segregation. Segregation at source, and Information and Education Campaigns were not fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management give priority for the repair and construction of school building in the preparation of the annual school board budget.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Please see discussion on Finding No. 5 of Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Treasurer and all other concerned be directed to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 by remitting taxes withheld within the period prescribed by regulation.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that job order personnel be compensated on a daily basis. Likewise, their job involve the human resource department in the selection and hiring of job-order personnel in order to ascertain the necessity of hiring these personnel.
observations.managementAction: Job Order was being studied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor should direct all concerned to immediately liquidate the outstanding cash advances for Petty Cash Fund and to stop granting additional cash advances to those accountable officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Petty Cash is centralized at the Office of the City Treasurer, but the previous grants to various accountable officials were not yet liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor and the City Treasurer should direct the City Accountant to stop granting cash advances to any accountable officer without specific purpose/s and granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances have been properly liquidated. Also require the maintenance of cashbooks in conformity with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer had already liquidated his cash advances. The Assistant City Treasurer is currently liquidating her cash advances. Cashbooks were maintained by City and Assistant City Treasurers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor should limit its appropriations and disbursements strictly within the limit prescribed by Section 325 (a) of RA 7160 and the guidelines provided in Local Budget Circular No. 98.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the foregoing be given preferential attention in order to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements, and to strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 13, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor should direct the City Assessor to update the real property assessment roll and prepare/send the Real Property Tax Order of Payment (RPTOP) to individual taxpayers to increase collections of real property taxes and apply remedies provided in the Local Government Code on Real Property Taxation.
observations.managementAction: The recorded RPT income for CY 2016 is P6,233,606.04 which is higher compared with that of the previous year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor should direct the head of the EEDMO to intensify its collection activities by exhausting all means to compel the delinquent occupants/stallholders to pay what is due to the government, and to provide assistance to the EEDMO in the enforcement of collection measures.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor, City Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly implement the provisions of Section 271 of RA 7160 and Section 5.06 of COA Circular No. 93-396 on the remittance within the prescribed period of the RPT shares of concerned barangays.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management draw a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the unliquidated cash advances to their proper asset accounts; exert extra efforts in securing information/data to pinpoint responsibility/liability to the untraced CY 2006 balances as well as the negative balances in the accounts and to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to come up with correct account balance.
observations.managementAction: The City intends to implement the recommendation but could not reclassify the accounts because the account Advances to Officers and Employees does not exist in the CY eNGAS. The balance was broken down in sub groups.
observations.reason: The complexity of the Prior years’ undocumented and negative cash balances prevented resolution of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor direct all concerned officials and employees to strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 114 and 43 of the New Government Accounting System, Vols. I and II, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Purchases were coursed thru the inventory account, except those purchases through reimbursements.
observations.reason: Procurement is not yet centralized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor should limit its PS appropriations to its limit and implement the No Fillip-up of vacant position policy to reduce the PS appropriations to its limit.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officials of the government strictly adhere with the rules and regulations set forth in COA Memorandum No. 87-492 as amended by COA Memorandum No. 98-016 dated June 16, 1998.
observations.managementAction: The installed 13 billboards were not the strictly prescribed ones.
observations.reason: Management was not able to fully comply with the regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to look into the covering disbursement vouchers for the recorded check disbursements totaling P75,852,764.62. Require the City Accountant to regularly prepare bank reconciliation statements, draw a journal entry voucher to record valid reconciling items, and cause the review and analysis of the cash accounts per books which are non-existing.
observations.managementAction: The accountant had reconciled some items in Bank Reconciliation Statements and some items were taken up in the books.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the remaining reconciling items is on going; the task was not finished.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accountant prepare and submit all the individual and consolidated barangay trial balance and financial statements as required in Section 122 of P.D. 1445, Section 347 of RA 7160 and COA Circular Nos. 93-396 and 99-005.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant prepared the individual and consolidated barangay trial balance and reports as required.
observations.reason: Late submission of paid vouchers and payrolls of various barangays caused the delay in preparation of individual trial balance and other financial reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor should direct the City Treasurer to immediately remit to the Bureau of Treasury thru the BFP Account D2531 the amount of P1,585,993.88 and stop the collection of fire code revenues in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-188.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to strictly adhere to DO No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005.
observations.managementAction: The city has already closed 2 accounts out of the 5 accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City General Services Officer strictly comply the provisions of Section 39(2) of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Titles of the land purchased were not yet transferred in the name of the City of Calbayog. The City General Services Officer stated that some documents needed for the transfer of titles are lacking, notable of which are the Certificates Authorizing Registration.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the necessity of the services of the JO personnel as well as the availability of cash to pay for their services should be carefully evaluated before the contract is issued in order to avoid cash overdrafts, accumulation of payables charged to prior year’s adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Hiring of Job Order was being studied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to Section 43 of the General Provisions of RA 9970 and rescind the Memorandum Agreement entered into by the City with private banks/lending institutions to avoid additional workload and overhead expenses.
observations.managementAction: Deductions for payments of loans from private lending institutions were still being made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Hon. City Mayor thru the City Treasurer enforce the COA Order of Execution (COE) by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the person liable in satisfaction of the disallowance totaling P581,608.50.
observations.managementAction: Installment payments were being made by some liable persons.
observations.reason: Please see discussion on Finding No. 11 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City strictly comply with the prescribed rules and regulations on the remittance of taxes withheld to avoid penalties imposed by the BIR.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The city was not able to remit all withheld taxes.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned agency officials to reclassify to the proper accounts the ‘for reclassification’ amount, verify and adjust those SL with abnormal balances totaling P9,448,299.19 and to exert extra effort to identify details of the unreconciled SL accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management is still reconciling the records and intends to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: Reconciliation process is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned agency officials to see to it that controls are in place and that expenditures should be limited to the actual available resources during the year. Prepare annually a budget and program of expenditures based on prioritized activities and a realistic estimate of income.
observations.managementAction: The City Budget Office provides a list of obligations to be accrued at year-end, then the City Accounting Office take it up to the books. A reconciliation process was to be implemented.
observations.reason: Controls were designed but not fully working.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of granting cash advances to persons other than the officers duly designated as disbursing officers. Require all heads of offices and other personnel with outstanding cash advances for petty expenses to immediately liquidate the full amount. For emergency purposes a petty cash fund may be granted to each department head subject to strict compliance with existing COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has issued demand letters to accountable officers with outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: Supposed controls were placed but not fully implemented.
